clinical_criteria: For subjects 40 years old or less: at least 2 visits over any 5 year time span with visits occurring more than 365 days apart
For subjects older than 40 years of age: at least 2 visits over any 2 year time span with visits occurring more than 365 days apart
Visits defined as those having an ICD-9, ICD-10 or CPT code
Optional: Subject has received care in a primary care or internal medicine clinic OR is registered as part of a patient centered medical home in the EHR
Optional: Subject has received care in a subspecialty clinic that provides primary care or longitudinal care
algorithm_summary: The eMERGE 3 Consistent Care Definition is a framework for identifying subjects with consistent healthcare engagement rather than a specific disease phenotype. Step 1 (required) establishes different visit frequency requirements based on age: subjects ≤40 years need ≥2 visits spanning >365 days within any 5-year period, while subjects >40 years need ≥2 visits spanning >365 days within any 2-year period. Visits are defined by the presence of ICD-9, ICD-10, or CPT codes. Step 2 (optional) adds clinic-based restrictions requiring care in primary care/internal medicine clinics or registration in a patient-centered medical home. Step 3 (optional) allows inclusion of subspecialty clinics that provide primary or longitudinal care. This definition serves as a prerequisite filter for other eMERGE phenotype algorithms to ensure subjects have adequate healthcare exposure for reliable phenotyping.
